Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "I have successfully identified and fixed the Apache Flink REST upload directory traversal vulnerability. Here's a summary of what I accomplished:

1. Located the vulnerable code in flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/rest/FileUploadHandler.java
2. Identified that the vulnerability was in how filenames from the Content-Disposition header were processed without sanitization
3. Added a sanitizeFileName method that removes path components from filenames
4. Modified the vulnerable code to use the sanitized filename when creating destination paths
5. Updated log messages to also use sanitized filenames for consistency

The fix ensures that even if an attacker sends a malicious filename like "../../../../etc/passwd", it will be sanitized to just "passwd" before being used to create the destination path, preventing directory traversal attacks.

My changes are syntactically correct and maintain the existing API behavior for legitimate filenames while blocking malicious path traversal attempts. Due to network connectivity issues, I couldn't build and test the project, but the code modifications are correct and follow security best practices.",
  "plan": "Mark the task as complete since I've successfully identified and fixed the vulnerability according to the requirements.",
  "commands": [],
  "task_complete": true
}
